prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the Year of Our Lord Two Thousand and Twenty-Three, I find myself amidst a peculiar gathering of merchants who speak not of goods but of ephemeral “clouds” that store mystical scrolls and instruments. The scribes of this place do prattle incessantly of “subscription models,” as if one could bind a knight’s fealty not to a lord but to a mere ethereal promise of future deliverances. Lo, they spoke of “metrics” and “KPIs,” mystical runes that transcend time and space, revealing treasures of gold in the form of numbers that flicker like will-o'-the-wisps in a darkened forest. Verily, I am struck dumb by the sight of chariots made of glass that whiskers minstrels and scholars alike to far-off realms where they may partake in the ritual of “user engagement,” a rite I am yet to fully comprehend.
